heading: Definitions

children.label: A
children.text: the primary financial regulatory agency, as defined in ;section 5301 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the National Credit Union Administration, with respect to any insured credit union under the Federal Credit Union Act ( et seq.); and12 U.S.C. 1751

children.label: C
children.text: the Board of Governors, with respect to organizations operating under section 25A of the Federal Reserve Act (), and any other financial institution engaged in a designated activity.12 U.S.C. 611

children.heading: Designated activity
children.label: 2 Designated activity
children.text: The term “designated activity” means a payment, clearing, or settlement activity that the Council has designated as systemically important under .section 5463 of this title

children.heading: Designated clearing entity
children.label: 3 Designated clearing entity
children.text: The term “designated clearing entity” means a designated financial market utility that is a derivatives clearing organization registered under section 5b of the Commodity Exchange Act () or a clearing agency registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission under section 17A of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ().7 U.S.C. 7a–115 U.S.C. 78q–1

children.heading: Designated financial market utility
children.label: 4 Designated financial market utility
children.text: The term “designated financial market utility” means a financial market utility that the Council has designated as systemically important under .section 5463 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a depository institution, as defined in ;section 1813 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a branch or agency of a foreign bank, as defined in ;section 3101 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: an organization operating under section 25 or 25A of the Federal Reserve Act ( and 611 through 631);12 U.S.C. 601–604a

children.label: iv
children.text: a credit union, as defined in section 101 of the Federal Credit Union Act ();12 U.S.C. 17521See References in Text note below.1

children.label: v
children.text: a broker or dealer, as defined in section 3 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ();15 U.S.C. 78c

children.label: vi
children.text: an investment company, as defined in ;section 80a–3 of title 15

children.label: vii
children.text: an insurance company, as defined in ;section 80a–2 of title 15

children.label: viii
children.text: an investment adviser, as defined in ;section 80b–2 of title 15

children.label: ix
children.text: a futures commission merchant, commodity trading advisor, or commodity pool operator, as defined in section 1a of the Commodity Exchange Act (); and7 U.S.C. 1a

children.label: x
children.text: any company engaged in activities that are financial in nature or incidental to a financial activity, as described in section 4 of the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 ().12 U.S.C. 1843(k)

children.heading: Exclusions
children.label: B Exclusions
children.text: The term “financial institution” does not include designated contract markets, registered futures associations, swap data repositories, and swap execution facilities registered under the Commodity Exchange Act ( et seq.), or national securities exchanges, national securities associations, alternative trading systems, securities information processors solely with respect to the activities of the entity as a securities information processor, security-based swap data repositories, and swap execution facilities registered under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ( et seq.), or designated clearing entities, provided that the exclusions in this subparagraph apply only with respect to the activities that require the entity to be so registered.7 U.S.C. 115 U.S.C. 78a

children.heading: Inclusion
children.label: A Inclusion
children.text: The term “financial market utility” means any person that manages or operates a multilateral system for the purpose of transferring, clearing, or settling payments, securities, or other financial transactions among financial institutions or between financial institutions and the person.

children.label: i
children.text: designated contract markets, registered futures associations, swap data repositories, and swap execution facilities registered under the Commodity Exchange Act ( et seq.), or national securities exchanges, national securities associations, alternative trading systems, security-based swap data repositories, and swap execution facilities registered under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ( et seq.), solely by reason of their providing facilities for comparison of data respecting the terms of settlement of securities or futures transactions effected on such exchange or by means of any electronic system operated or controlled by such entities, provided that the exclusions in this clause apply only with respect to the activities that require the entity to be so registered; and7 U.S.C. 115 U.S.C. 78a

children.label: ii
children.text: any broker, dealer, transfer agent, or investment company, or any futures commission merchant, introducing broker, commodity trading advisor, or commodity pool operator, solely by reason of functions performed by such institution as part of brokerage, dealing, transfer agency, or investment company activities, or solely by reason of acting on behalf of a financial market utility or a participant therein in connection with the furnishing by the financial market utility of services to its participants or the use of services of the financial market utility by its participants, provided that services performed by such institution do not constitute critical risk management or processing functions of the financial market utility.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “payment, clearing, or settlement activity” means an activity carried out by 1 or more financial institutions to facilitate the completion of financial transactions, but shall not include any offer or sale of a security under the Securities Act of 1933 ( et seq.), or any quotation, order entry, negotiation, or other pre-trade activity or execution activity.15 U.S.C. 77a

children.label: i
children.text: funds transfers;

children.label: ii
children.text: securities contracts;

children.label: iii
children.text: contracts of sale of a commodity for future delivery;

children.label: iv
children.text: forward contracts;

children.label: v
children.text: repurchase agreements;

children.label: vi
children.text: swaps;

children.label: vii
children.text: security-based swaps;

children.label: viii
children.text: swap agreements;

children.label: ix
children.text: security-based swap agreements;

children.label: x
children.text: foreign exchange contracts;

children.label: xi
children.text: financial derivatives contracts; and

children.label: xii
children.text: any similar transaction that the Council determines to be a financial transaction for purposes of this subchapter.

children.label: i
children.text: the calculation and communication of unsettled financial transactions between counterparties;

children.label: ii
children.text: the netting of transactions;

children.label: iii
children.text: provision and maintenance of trade, contract, or instrument information;

children.label: iv
children.text: the management of risks and activities associated with continuing financial transactions;

children.label: v
children.text: transmittal and storage of payment instructions;

children.label: vi
children.text: the movement of funds;

children.label: vii
children.text: the final settlement of financial transactions; and

children.label: viii
children.text: other similar functions that the Council may determine.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: D Exclusion
children.text: Payment, clearing, and settlement activities shall not include public reporting of swap transaction data under section 727 or 763(i) of the Wall Street Transparency and Accountability Act of 2010.

children.label: i
children.text: The Securities and Exchange Commission, with respect to a designated financial market utility that is a clearing agency registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission, with respect to a designated financial market utility that is a derivatives clearing organization registered with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission.

children.label: iii
children.text: The appropriate Federal banking agency, with respect to a designated financial market utility that is an institution described in .section 1813(q) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: The Board of Governors, with respect to a designated financial market utility that is otherwise not subject to the jurisdiction of any agency listed in clauses (i), (ii), and (iii).

children.heading: Multiple agency jurisdiction
children.label: B Multiple agency jurisdiction
children.text: If a designated financial market utility is subject to the jurisdictional supervision of more than 1 agency listed in subparagraph (A), then such agencies should agree on 1 agency to act as the Supervisory Agency, and if such agencies cannot agree on which agency has primary jurisdiction, the Council shall decide which agency is the Supervisory Agency for purposes of this subchapter.

children.heading: Systemically important and systemic importance
children.label: 9 Systemically important and systemic importance
children.text: The terms “systemically important” and “systemic importance” mean a situation where the failure of or a disruption to the functioning of a financial market utility or the conduct of a payment, clearing, or settlement activity could create, or increase, the risk of significant liquidity or credit problems spreading among financial institutions or markets and thereby threaten the stability of the financial system of the United States.
